Off Calendar Jul-27-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
John L. Kortum, Esq. of ARCHER NORRIS (925-930-6600), appearing for defendant / moving party AutoZone West, Inc.
Grant E. Walters, Esq., (415-332-4262), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Dennis Henry Schechinger

RULING:
Defendant AutoZone West, Inc.'s motion to strike portions of plaintiff's complaint. Argued and the Court adopted the tentative ruling: Defendant's motion to strike is denied. The complaint pleads sufficient facts supporting the prayer for punitive damages. Defendant did not cite, nor is the court aware of any, authority requiring that a plaintiff allege facts supporting a request for punitive damages that are specific to each of the defendants sued. A request for punitive damages may be based on a non-fraud products liability claim provided that sufficient facts showing malice, fraud and/or oppression are alleged. Defendant must answer the complaint within 10 days of the hearing date. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7707 =(220/HEK)
